skill.name: tdd-orchestrator
skill.description: Guides disciplined test-driven development with red-green-refactor cycles, test strategy planning, workflow coordination, and quality metrics.
skill.summary: Coordinate TDD practices, testing strategy, and red-green-refactor workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found prose-only TDD guidance in SKILL.md. No shell execution, cryptographic implementation, system enumeration, network access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Pattern
low_findings.description: The flagged line tells the assistant to open a Markdown resource when detailed examples are needed. It does not invoke a shell, run backticks, or pass user input to an external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is natural-language file-reading guidance, and the repository contains no executable helper files. No command execution syntax or injection vector is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are ordinary words and phrases in TDD documentation, such as master, Continuous Integration, Domain-Driven Design, and example workflow titles. No cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is descriptive Markdown content, not code. There is no hash function, cipher selection, key handling, or crypto API usage to evaluate.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss measuring TDD cycle time, optimizing test pyramids, test execution, and AI-assisted test generation. They do not request host enumeration, file discovery, credential collection, or environment inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is software testing methodology. No system commands, network probes, filesystem scans, or secret discovery instructions appear near these lines.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose TDD discipline when requirements, tests, and refactoring work drift apart. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan test-first workflows and quality checks.
actual_capabilities: Plans red-green-refactor workflows for new features and bug fixes.
Designs unit, integration, contract, and end-to-end testing strategies.
Coordinates multi-agent or multi-role testing responsibilities.
Identifies TDD anti-patterns such as test-after development and brittle tests.
Defines quality gates, coverage goals, and feedback loop metrics.
Guides legacy code characterization before refactoring work.
limitations: It provides guidance and workflow structure, not automatic test execution.
It does not include bundled scripts, test runners, or framework plugins.
It needs project context to produce precise test plans.
It cannot guarantee coverage or correctness without reviewing the codebase.

use_cases.title: Launch a New Feature with TDD
use_cases.description: Plan tests before implementation, define acceptance checks, and keep refactoring inside a safe feedback cycle.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Testing Practice
use_cases.description: Create shared expectations for test levels, quality gates, review checks, and continuous improvement metrics.

use_cases.title: Refactor Legacy Code Safely
use_cases.description: Build characterization tests, identify safe refactoring steps, and protect behavior during incremental cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Start a TDD Cycle
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a red-green-refactor cycle for this feature. Include the first failing test, minimum implementation, and refactor checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Test Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this requirement and propose a balanced test strategy across unit, integration, contract, and end-to-end tests.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Testing Roles
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-role TDD workflow for this project. Assign responsibilities for test design, implementation, review, and quality gates.

prompt_templates.title: Govern TDD Adoption
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a TDD governance plan for multiple teams. Include standards, metrics, anti-pattern detection, and continuous improvement reviews.

output_examples.input: Plan TDD for a password reset feature.
output: Defines acceptance tests for valid, expired, and reused reset links.
Starts with a failing unit test for token validation behavior.
Adds integration checks for email delivery and persistence boundaries.
Schedules refactoring after the smallest passing implementation.

output_examples.input: Improve testing for a slow service test suite.
output: Separates fast unit tests from slower integration tests.
Identifies brittle shared fixtures and database state coupling.
Proposes feedback loop targets and CI quality gates.
Recommends incremental refactoring protected by characterization tests.

output_examples.input: Create a team TDD operating model.
output: Maps roles for developers, reviewers, and test strategy owners.
Defines review questions for test-first evidence and refactoring safety.
Adds metrics for cycle time, coverage value, and escaped defects.

best_practices: Provide requirements, existing test structure, and framework constraints before asking for a TDD plan.
Ask for the smallest failing test first, then expand coverage after behavior is clear.
Use the skill to review test quality, not only test quantity or coverage percentages.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for implementation code before defining the expected failing test.
Do not treat coverage targets as proof that important behavior is tested.
Do not apply one testing pyramid rule without considering product risk and architecture.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides test planning, workflow design, and review practices. Separate tools are needed to execute tests.

faq.question: Which languages does it support?
faq.answer: It discusses TDD patterns for common stacks, including Java, C#, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Go.

faq.question: Can it help with legacy systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can plan characterization tests and incremental refactoring steps before behavior changes are made.

faq.question: Is it useful for teams already using CI?
faq.answer: Yes. It can connect TDD practice to CI feedback loops, quality gates, and review standards.

faq.question: Can it generate test cases from requirements?
faq.answer: Yes. It can propose test cases and test levels when requirements and constraints are supplied.

faq.question: Does it replace human test strategy review?
faq.answer: No. It supports planning and critique, but teams should verify the plan against real code and product risk.
